page: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768g-5760s is a high-capacity instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 96 vCPUs built on the AMD x86_64 architecture. The system provides 96 physical cores with a single thread per core configuration. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is comprised of a 5760 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

A key architectural characteristic of this server is its shared CPU allocation, which introduces potential resource contention compared to dedicated-core instances. However, this shared model offers a qualitative cost-efficiency advantage for workloads requiring massive memory and storage capacities without needing continuous, isolated CPU performance. The combination of 768.0 GB of RAM and 5760 GB of local NVMe SSD provides high resource density for data-heavy operations.

Given its substantial memory footprint and large local NVMe storage, this server is configured for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. Suitable use cases include large-scale databases, data caching layers, big data analytics, and memory-optimized enterprise applications that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling.
description: Vultr vx1-m-96c-768g-5760s is a memory-optimized instance from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 96 AMD x86_64 vCPUs with 96 physical cores and a single thread per core. The server is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 5760 GB local NVMe SSD. A primary architectural tradeoff is its shared CPU allocation, which makes it highly cost-efficient for resource-dense deployments but subject to potential CPU resource sharing. This configuration is suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive caching systems, and data analytics workloads that require massive memory and storage capacities without requiring dedicated CPU isolation. The inclusion of one complimentary public IPv4 address completes the network configuration, making it a robust option for memory-heavy enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-capacity instance combining a 768 GB memory footprint and 5.7 TB NVMe storage under a shared CPU allocation model.
